description: A social media posting server that manages user authentication, tweet creation and management, social interactions (retweets, comments), and user follow relationships.

tools.name: login
tools.description: Authenticate a user with credentials and establish an active session for performing posting operations.

success.description: Indicates whether the login attempt was successful

session_token.description: Session token for maintaining authenticated state, returned only if login is successful

username.description: The username of the authenticated user account, returned only if login is successful

tools.name: logout
tools.description: Terminate the current user session and invalidate the authentication token.

success.description: Indicates whether the logout operation completed successfully

tools.name: check_login_status
tools.description: Verify whether a user session is currently active and authenticated.

is_authenticated.description: Indicates whether a user is currently authenticated with an active session

username.description: The username of the authenticated user account, returned only if a session is active

tools.name: create_tweet
tools.description: Publish a new tweet with optional hashtags and user mentions for the authenticated user.

content.description: The text content of the tweet to be published

hashtags.description: Optional list of hashtags to include in the tweet. Each hashtag must start with # (e.g., ['#python', '#coding']). If not provided, no hashtags are added.

mentions.description: Optional list of usernames to mention in the tweet. Each mention must start with @ (e.g., ['@alice', '@bob']). If not provided, no mentions are added.

tweet_id.description: The unique identifier of the created tweet, used for subsequent operations like retrieval, updates, or deletion

username.description: The username of the authenticated user account who authored the tweet

content.description: The text content of the published tweet

hashtags.description: List of hashtags included in the tweet

mentions.description: List of usernames mentioned in the tweet

created_at.description: Timestamp when the tweet was created, in ISO 8601 format

tools.name: get_tweet
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific tweet including engagement metrics.

tweet_id.description: The unique identifier of the tweet to retrieve. Can be obtained from create_tweet, list_tweets, or search_tweets operations.
The unique identifier of the tweet

username.description: The username of the authenticated user account who authored the tweet

content.description: The text content of the tweet

hashtags.description: List of hashtags included in the tweet

mentions.description: List of usernames mentioned in the tweet

created_at.description: Timestamp when the tweet was created, in ISO 8601 format

retweet_count.description: Total number of times this tweet has been retweeted by other users

comment_count.description: Total number of comments posted on this tweet

tools.name: list_tweets
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of tweets with optional filtering by username or followed users.

username.description: Optional username to filter tweets by a specific author. If not provided and user is authenticated, returns tweets from followed users. If not authenticated, returns public tweets.

limit.description: Maximum number of tweets to return in the response. Defaults to 5.

offset.description: Number of tweets to skip for pagination, enabling retrieval of subsequent pages. Defaults to 0.

tweet_id.description: The unique identifier of the tweet, used for subsequent operations like retrieval, updates, or deletion

username.description: The username of the authenticated user account who authored the tweet

content.description: The text content of the tweet

created_at.description: Timestamp when the tweet was created, in ISO 8601 format

retweet_count.description: Total number of times this tweet has been retweeted by other users

comment_count.description: Total number of comments posted on this tweet

tweets.description: List of tweet summaries matching the filter criteria. Tweet IDs can be used with get_tweet for full details.

tools.name: search_tweets
tools.description: Search for tweets containing a specific keyword in their content or hashtags.

query.description: The search keyword to match against tweet content or hashtags

limit.description: Maximum number of matching tweets to return in the response. Defaults to 5.

tweet_id.description: The unique identifier of the tweet, used for subsequent operations like retrieval, updates, or deletion

username.description: The username of the authenticated user account who authored the tweet

content.description: The text content of the tweet

hashtags.description: List of hashtags included in the tweet

created_at.description: Timestamp when the tweet was created, in ISO 8601 format

retweet_count.description: Total number of times this tweet has been retweeted by other users

comment_count.description: Total number of comments posted on this tweet

tweets.description: List of tweets matching the search query. Tweet IDs can be used with get_tweet for full details.

tools.name: update_tweet
tools.description: Modify an existing tweet's content, hashtags, or mentions. Only the original author can update their own tweets.

tweet_id.description: The unique identifier of the tweet to update. Can be obtained from create_tweet, list_tweets, or search_tweets operations.

content.description: Optional new text content to replace the existing tweet content

hashtags.description: Optional new list of hashtags to replace existing hashtags. Each hashtag must start with #.

mentions.description: Optional new list of usernames to replace existing mentions. Each mention must start with @.

tweet_id.description: The unique identifier of the updated tweet

success.description: Indicates whether the update operation completed successfully

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the tweet was last updated, in ISO 8601 format

updated_fields.description: List of field names that were modified in this update operation

tools.name: delete_tweet
tools.description: Permanently remove a tweet from the system. Only the original author can delete their own tweets.

tweet_id.description: The unique identifier of the tweet to delete. Can be obtained from create_tweet, list_tweets, or search_tweets operations.
The unique identifier of the deleted tweet

success.description: Indicates whether the deletion operation completed successfully

message.description: Confirmation message describing the result of the deletion operation

tools.name: retweet
tools.description: Share an existing tweet to the authenticated user's followers by retweeting it.

tweet_id.description: The unique identifier of the tweet to retweet. Can be obtained from get_tweet, list_tweets, or search_tweets operations.

success.description: Indicates whether the retweet operation completed successfully

message.description: Status message describing the result (e.g., 'Successfully retweeted' or 'Already retweeted')

retweeted_at.description: Timestamp when the retweet was created, in ISO 8601 format. Only returned if the retweet was successful.

tools.name: add_comment
tools.description: Post a comment on an existing tweet for the authenticated user.

tweet_id.description: The unique identifier of the tweet to comment on. Can be obtained from get_tweet, list_tweets, or search_tweets operations.

comment_content.description: The text content of the comment to be posted

comment_id.description: The unique identifier of the created comment

tweet_id.description: The unique identifier of the tweet that was commented on

username.description: The username of the authenticated user account who authored the comment

comment_content.description: The text content of the posted comment

created_at.description: Timestamp when the comment was created, in ISO 8601 format

tools.name: get_tweet_comments
tools.description: Retrieve all comments posted on a specific tweet with pagination support.

tweet_id.description: The unique identifier of the tweet to retrieve comments for. Can be obtained from get_tweet, list_tweets, or search_tweets operations.

limit.description: Maximum number of comments to return in the response. Defaults to 50.

offset.description: Number of comments to skip for pagination, enabling retrieval of subsequent pages. Defaults to 0.

comment_id.description: The unique identifier of the comment

tweet_id.description: The unique identifier of the tweet that was commented on

username.description: The username of the authenticated user account who authored the comment

comment_content.description: The text content of the comment

created_at.description: Timestamp when the comment was created, in ISO 8601 format

comments.description: List of all comments posted on the specified tweet

tools.name: follow_user
tools.description: Establish a following relationship with another user to see their tweets in your feed.

username.description: The username of the user account to follow

success.description: Indicates whether the follow operation completed successfully

message.description: Status message describing the result (e.g., 'Successfully followed' or 'Already following')

followed_at.description: Timestamp when the follow relationship was established, in ISO 8601 format. Only returned if the follow was successful.

tools.name: unfollow_user
tools.description: Remove an existing following relationship with another user.

username.description: The username of the user account to unfollow

success.description: Indicates whether the unfollow operation completed successfully

message.description: Status message describing the result (e.g., 'Successfully unfollowed' or 'Not following')

tools.name: list_following
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of all users that a specific user or the authenticated user is following.

username.description: Optional username to retrieve the following list for. If not provided, returns the following list for the current authenticated user.

limit.description: Maximum number of users to return in the response. Defaults to 50.

offset.description: Number of users to skip for pagination, enabling retrieval of subsequent pages. Defaults to 0.

username.description: The username of the user account being followed

followed_at.description: Timestamp when the follow relationship was established, in ISO 8601 format

following.description: List of all users that the specified user is following

tools.name: list_followers
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of all users that follow a specific user or the authenticated user.

username.description: Optional username to retrieve the followers list for. If not provided, returns the followers list for the current authenticated user.

limit.description: Maximum number of users to return in the response. Defaults to 50.

offset.description: Number of users to skip for pagination, enabling retrieval of subsequent pages. Defaults to 0.

username.description: The username of the user account that is following

followed_at.description: Timestamp when the follow relationship was established, in ISO 8601 format

followers.description: List of all users that follow the specified user

tools.name: get_user_stats
tools.description: Retrieve comprehensive statistics and metrics for a specific user or the authenticated user.

username.description: Optional username to retrieve statistics for. If not provided, returns statistics for the current authenticated user.
The username of the user account for which statistics are provided

tweet_count.description: Total number of tweets posted by the user

following_count.description: Total number of users that this user is following

followers_count.description: Total number of users that follow this user

retweet_count.description: Total number of retweets made by the user
